metadata.claimIdCollision: claimId 'c1' is used by two distinct claims (volunteer ELIGIBILITY_REQUIREMENT and health-professionals OFFERS_OBSERVERSHIP). Downstream dedupe must disambiguate.

unfollowedSignals: Physician-to-Physician Observer Application Request at TGH Main — navigation hint of an observer pathway on health-professionals page; A1/A2 did not retrieve the linked application page to confirm whether the program is open to external/IMG physicians or restricted to credentialed/affiliated staff.
unresolveds: claimId collision: two distinct claims share claimId 'c1' (one from https://tgh.org/volunteer ELIGIBILITY_REQUIREMENT, one from https://tgh.org/health-professionals OFFERS_OBSERVERSHIP). Not a public-safety issue but a structural ID violation; downstream merge must rename one before persistence.

Observer pathway signal ('Physician-to-Physician Observer Application Request at TGH Main') is correctly held at CAUTION_SAFE_INTERNAL_REVIEW; eligibility (IMG vs affiliated physicians only), duration, fees, and contact remain unknown and require A4 narrow follow-up on the application request page.
verdictSummary: Ledger contains zero PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claims, which is the correct outcome given evidence: TGH's public surfaces show a non-medical volunteer program, careers/GME content, and a navigation-only observer pathway hint correctly held at CAUTION_SAFE_INTERNAL_REVIEW. No scope conflicts, no overclaims, no family/lane mismatches. Two non-safety issues noted: claimId 'c1' collision and an unfollowed observer-application signal warranting A4 narrow recovery, not a publish blocker.
